title: PlaneSense | Number One Private Lift Provider By BJT
meta_description: The Business Jet Traveler Survey of private flight operators and their client satisfaction of PlaneSense aircraft owners.

h1: PlaneSense Ranked #1 Lift Provider by Private Flyers!
h2: Private Flyers Seek Pilatus Aircraft
The Benefits of PlaneSense
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution
h3: Choosing the Right Solution For You

body_text:
PlaneSense Ranked #1 Lift Provider by Private Flyers!
Each year Business Jet Traveler magazine (BJT) surveys its readers on a variety of topics pertaining to private flying, including the evaluation of lift providers. Twelve operators made the 2024 list, but the PlaneSense® fractional program came out on top in five of the six categories, earning the highest score of 4.0 in three categories:
• Overall satisfaction – 4.0
• Customer service – 3.89
• Value for price paid – 3.78
• Peak day policies – 4.0
• Availability of aircraft on short notice – 4.0
For 30 years, PlaneSense has been committed to providing clients with world class safety, service, and value.
Private Flyers Seek Pilatus Aircraft
The PlaneSense® fleet currently features 43 Pilatus PC-12 turboprops and 14 PC-24 jets. Coincidentally, the PC-12 was ranked as the most sought-after single engine turboprop by BJT survey participants, and the PC-24 jet moved up to second place in the light jet category. Fractional owners have long recognized the aircraft in the PlaneSense® fleet for their versatility, reliability, large cabins, comfort, interior luggage space, and efficiency.
Read More
The Benefits of PlaneSense
The PlaneSense Advantage
World class service, unmatched reliability, state-of-the-art aircraft, expert maintenance, and an uncompromising commitment to safety – all for a fraction of the cost of other programs.
- Why Fly PlaneSense
Your Fleet
The highly sought-after Pilatus PC-12 turboprop and PC-24 jet offer the most advanced technology and safety features, plus remarkable reliability, spacious cabins, and luxurious touches.
- Your Fleet
Where You Can Fly
The PlaneSense
®
program and fleet offer access to thousands of airports on both U.S. coasts, plus Canada, Bermuda, the Bahamas, the Caribbean, Central America, and Mexico.
- Service Area Map
Commitment to Safety
Infused into every aspect of operations, safety is always the highest priority for the PlaneSense
®
team, earning the program Platinum Elite status, the top ARGUS safety rating.
- Safety And Service
Understanding Fractional Ownership
Download this helpful guide to learn how the fractional management and cost structure makes flying privately a valuable, convenient, easy, and reliable private flight solution.
- Fractional Aircraft Program
Choosing the Right Solution For You
With numerous options on the market, how do you know which private flight solution is right for you? Download this informative guide to compare fractional and other programs.
- Guide to Fractional Ownership
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution
Tell us about your private travel needs and our team will provide you with travel solutions that are customized to fit you. For career opportunities and questions, please go to our
careers section
.
For career questions, email
careers@planesense.com

title: 30th Anniversary Celebration | PlaneSense
meta_description: 30th Anniversary! PlaneSense has soared above industry standards, delivering unrivaled expertise and a legacy of excellence.

h1: Fractional Ownership that Stands the Test of Time
Celebrating 30 Years of Excellence
Innovative Private Aviation Solutions
A Seamless Source for Global Travel
Thirty Years of Trusted Partnerships
h2: Fractional Ownership
CobaltPass Jet Card
Arrive Closer to Your Destination
Welcome to Europe, Courtesy of Jetfly
Travel Farther with the PlaneSense Sourcing Solution
A Team Focused On Safety and Service
Looking Back at PlaneSense History
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution
h3: Own a Share in A Private Plane

body_text:
Fractional Ownership that Stands the Test of Time
Celebrating 30 Years of Excellence
In private aviation, longevity is not measured merely by years, but by the enduring trust cultivated every day in the skies and on the ground. For three decades, PlaneSense has soared above industry standards, delivering unrivaled expertise, steadfast service, and a legacy of excellence.
In the dynamic and highly competitive world of aviation, longevity is rare. As market dynamics shift, regulations become more rigorous, and technology advances at a rapid pace, many operators have entered the fractional market with promise, only to leave quietly when confronted with the challenges of sustained operation. The remarkable endurance of PlaneSense stems from disciplined management, ongoing innovation, and a dedicated commitment to both safety and service.
“When we launched PlaneSense three decades ago, our vision was to offer a more intelligent and highly personal approach to private flying. We believed clients deserved a sophisticated aircraft but also a trusted partner dedicated to fulfilling their flight needs with a cost-effective, safe, and service-focused solution. Thirty years later, that philosophy still drives every decision we make.”
George Antoniadis, Founder, President, and CEO, PlaneSense.
Trust your private flights to a program built on thirty years of operational excellence, where every flight reflects a commitment that truly stands the test of time.
Innovative Private Aviation Solutions
PlaneSense clients enjoy access to a comprehensive suite of innovative private flight programs and personalized service, designed to deliver flexibility, safety, and exceptional value every time you fly. Tested and refined over time, these programs have consistently proven to be reliable and enduring solutions, giving clients confidence in their choice of private aviation partner.
Fractional Ownership
Own a Share in A Private Plane
For 30 years, the PlaneSense fractional program has remained a top choice for discerning travelers, delivering the freedom and adaptability that private aviation clients demand. Owners benefit from flexible access to premium aircraft, all at a significantly lower cost compared to traditional charter or full ownership models.
Learn More About The PlaneSense Fractional Program
CobaltPass Jet Card
Private Flying On Your Terms
The CobaltPass Jet Card has redefined the jet card experience by offering unmatched flexibility, including no blackout dates and the ability to tailor flight hours and aircraft selection to individual preferences.
Learn More About the CobaltPass Jet Card
A Seamless Source for Global Travel
Flights to Thousands of Airports Across the U.S. and Beyond
Over three decades, PlaneSense has been committed to enhancing its service area to offer the most flexible travel opportunities for its clients. Through state-of-the-art aircraft, strategic partnerships and new flight solutions, PlaneSense has expanded its footprint, now connecting clients to destinations worldwide with seamless service and convenience.
Arrive Closer to Your Destination
The unique landing capabilities of the PlaneSense fleet allow access to four times more airports than most other private aviation programs. Thanks to the remarkable design of the PC-12, which can land on grass and dirt runways, as well as the versatile performance of both the PC-12 and PC-24 jet, clients can reach short runways, rural airports, and remote destinations that are inaccessible to larger aircraft. This means shorter commutes and the ability to travel directly to the heart of smaller communities in every corner of the continental U.S., as well as Canada, Bermuda, the Bahamas, the Caribbean, Central America, and Mexico. PlaneSense brings you closer to your destination, opening up a world of travel possibilities and convenience.
The PlaneSense Service Area
Welcome to Europe, Courtesy of Jetfly
Through an exclusive collaboration with Jetfly, PlaneSense fractional share owners can seamlessly access travel to Europe and Northern Africa, utilizing their flight hours on Jetfly’s fleet of Pilatus aircraft. Experience the exceptional service, uncompromising safety, and versatile capabilities that you expect from PlaneSense, but now in Europe.
Jetfly Service Area
Travel Farther with the PlaneSense Sourcing Solution
Introducing an exclusive service for PlaneSense clients. With the expertise and attentive support of the PlaneSense Integrated Operations Center team, you can enjoy dependable private flights throughout the U.S. and around the globe, including access to aircraft outside the PlaneSense fleet. This premium offering expands your options for both business and leisure travel, enabling you to utilize a broader range of aircraft and ensuring seamless, personalized service wherever your journey takes you.
Thirty Years of Trusted Partnerships
Since its inception in 1995, PlaneSense has built a legacy of excellence through enduring partnerships with Pilatus Aircraft Ltd, Pratt & Whitney Canada, and other esteemed business collaborators. The mutual trust and support from these relationships has been instrumental in providing clients with access to advanced, reliable aircraft and cutting-edge technology, ensuring the highest standards of safety and performance. By working hand-in-hand with industry leaders, PlaneSense is able to deliver innovative solutions and adapt swiftly to the evolving needs of private aviation.
A Team Focused On Safety and Service
Uncompromising dedication to safety is deeply ingrained at every level of PlaneSense and its sister company, Atlas Aircraft Center. The company adheres to rigorous operational standards and maintains a culture of continuous improvement, earning the prestigious ARGUS Platinum Elite Status as recognition of its exemplary safety practices. PlaneSense operates a state-of-the-art fleet of modern aircraft, which are maintained by expert technicians and flown by highly skilled pilots who undergo extensive, ongoing training to uphold the highest levels of safety and reliability for every flight.
Equally important is the team’s unwavering commitment to service. The entire team of 600 professionals is determined to go the extra mile for clients, making personalized care and attention a distinguishing feature of the PlaneSense experience. Their determination and dedication sets PlaneSense apart in the industry, ensuring that every client receives exceptional service on every journey.
Pilot Training
Atlas Aircraft Center
Integrated Operations Center
Looking Back at PlaneSense History
Founded on a passion for aviation and a belief in better solutions for private flights, PlaneSense has been a leading provider of private flight and fractional aircraft ownership for over 30 years. The second-oldest fractional program in the world has grown steadily and soundly and now operates the largest U.S. fleet of Pilatus PC-12 turboprops and PC-24 jets.
September 9, 1995
The PlaneSense fractional aircraft ownership program launches in Norwood, MA with the delivery of its first Pilatus PC-12, sn120, the 20
th
off the assembly line.
2007
PlaneSense and sister company, Atlas Aircraft Center move into its state-of-the-art headquarters in Portsmouth, NH.
2018
As the launch customer of the Pilatus PC-24 jet, PlaneSense takes delivery of N124AF.
2021
A new maintenance facility is established in Boulder City, NV. Out of area fees are eliminated for the PC-12 program across the U.S.
2023
Pilatus delivers its 2000
th
PC-12 to PlaneSense at a special event in Stans, Switzerland
2024
PC-24 out of area fees in the Western U.S. are eliminated, opening up the entire continental U.S. and parts of Canada to the clients across the PlaneSense fleet.
2025
Pilatus PC-12 NGX Level D Full Flight Simulator launched in Tampa, FL to enhance in-house pilot training program.
Collaboration with Jetfly launched in April
CobaltPass and CobaltPass+ Jet Cards launched in May
PlaneSense Sourcing Solution launched in August
PlaneSense celebrates 30 years of excellence

title: PlaneSense Aids Hurricane Dorian Relief Efforts in the Bahamas
meta_description: On September 16th, the PlaneSense team flew thousands of pounds of supplies to assist in the Hurricane Dorian relief effort for the Bahamas.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: PlaneSense Assists with Hurricane Dorian Relief Efforts in the Bahamas
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
PlaneSense Assists with Hurricane Dorian Relief Efforts in the Bahamas
News
•
September 19, 2019
The Bahamas have been an important part of the PlaneSense, Inc. story for decades. From the colleagues that have fueled our aircraft to those that have taken care of our pilots and everyone in between, countless people on the islands have made an impact on our fractional aircraft program. On September 16, we were able to give back by playing a small part in the recovery efforts after Hurricane Dorian. With the help of the PlaneSense team at the company’s headquarters, Captain Rob Nee and First Officer Eva Harden flew four trips in PC-12 to Freeport, Grand Bahama to assist the Miami Center Controllers and the NATCA Disaster Response Committee with Hurricane Dorian relief efforts. They transported 4,400 pounds of supplies including food, pet food, tents, cooking and cleaning supplies, water, and much more. They also delivered lunch to the airport staff, some of whom had lost their homes. Their high spirits amid the destruction are an inspiration. Please keep the Bahamas in your thoughts as recovery efforts continue.

title: GSAMA Pilots & Drivers Charity Golf Tournament | PlaneSense
meta_description: PlaneSense took part the 5th annual Pilots and Drivers Charity Golf Tournament in order to raise funds for STEM and NH aviation.
author: John Lafreniere

h1: 5th Annual GSAMA Pilots & Drivers Charity Golf Tournament
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
5th Annual GSAMA Pilots & Drivers Charity Golf Tournament
Behind the Scenes
•
October 10, 2019
On September 27th, aviation enthusiasts and golfers alike joined forces for the 5
th
Annual Pilots & Drivers Charity Golf Tournament at Pease Golf Course, organized by the Granite State Airport Management Association (GSAMA). The event netted $10,000 this year, thanks to sponsors and generous donations. Teams played 18 holes of scramble play, and prizes went to top 3 finishers, longest drives, and closest to the hole shots. The event has been a favored networking opportunity for many people in the industry.
History:
Started in 2014, the tournament has aimed to raise funds for aviation-based causes in New Hampshire. PlaneSense, Inc. has played a role in the tournament for the last several years, thanks to Lorri Badolato. Lorri, our Deputy General Counsel, GSAMA Board Member, and official PlaneSense golf enthusiast involved us in this great cause. PlaneSense was a sponsor for the event, and many of its team members volunteered. PlaneSense employees were also present in several teams.
Funding great programs:
The event proceeds often support programs such as the NH Aviation Museum’s RV-12 Program. The program purchase airplane kits for Manchester Vocational/Technical School students who build the aircraft as part of their hands-on training. Other funding recipients have included the ACE Academy camp through Winn Aero in Laconia, supporting CAD design software and construction kits for glider prototypes. The camps get youth involved in the aviation industry. Manchester Central Robotics club has also received funds through the golf tournament. Funding has also supported the Aviation Trust Fund, which assists privately owned airports in NH that get no federal funding.
Thank you to all of those who participated in this wonderful event.
GSAMA is “a group of aviation professionals committed to increasing awareness of the importance of aviation and supporting economic development for the State of New Hampshire. GSAMA serves New Hampshire’s aviation needs by enhancing access to the National Airspace System, promoting a support network for all airports in the State, and promoting legislation in support of aviation interests.”

title: Weather-Based Flight Cancellations: How PlaneSense Avoids It
meta_description: When it comes to avoiding frustrating weather-based flight cancellations, nothing gets the job done like private flight with PlaneSense.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: How Private Flight Avoids Weather-Based Flight Cancellations
h2: A Case Study in a Blizzard
Inclement Weather Won’t Stop Private Flight
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
How Private Flight Avoids Weather-Based Flight Cancellations
Industry Insights
•
December 19, 2017
Picture this: There’s a blizzard rolling in. Scores of airlines cancel flights preemptively, days before the snowflakes even begin to spiral down from the sky. You’re left to frantically try to rebook your reservations or find alternative travel arrangements at the last minute.
This is not a hypothetical situation. The March 13, 2017 blizzard caused the cancellation of over 9,000 commercial flights, impacting thousands upon thousands of travelers. Those who acquired private aircraft avoided the last minute stress and scramble, however. As winter weather begins to take shape again, scenarios like this will most likely happen many times across the country.
How can a storm that leads to commercial flight cancellations for thousands days before the storm even hits only slightly inconvenience a fractional jet ownership program like PlaneSense? Read on to learn more about how private flights avoid weather-based flight cancellations.
A Case Study in a Blizzard
Here’s how it went at PlaneSense, Inc.: As we watched the storm develop on weather monitoring system, we quickly began to contact owners who had scheduled flights that may be impacted by the weather conditions. Our
Flight Operations Center
(FOC) personnel worked with owners to adjust flight schedules and ensure everyone got to their intended destination before the storm hit in earnest, which would have impacted their travel plans.
Even on the day of the storm, our fleet of PC-12 aircraft took off early in the morning to pick up passengers and bring them to their destinations before the blizzard arrived.
Did we fly during the actual blizzard? No, because safety is one of our enduring priorities. Did we get our passengers where they needed to go, unlike the commercial airlines, cancelling flights well before a storm hits to avoid repositioning costs? Absolutely.
Inclement Weather Won’t Stop Private Flight
This is not an uncommon scenario for us. Come rain, hail, hurricane, or snow, PlaneSense makes arrangements to ensure our passengers get to where they need to go. It might mean a slight adjustment of your pickup and arrival times, but that’s a preferable alternative to being stranded or driving long distances  by car.
Because our Flight Operations Center is manned 24 hours a day by professionals who keep a close eye on the weather, we can react to storms well in advance and ensure we’ve worked with you to find an appropriate pickup time, and an alternative destination if your preferred airport is closed. That’s flexibility and a personal connection that airlines can’t and won’t provide.
To learn more about how you can fly in the winter and avoid weather-based flight cancellations with less headaches,
reach out to us today
.

title: Donate to Help PlaneSense Feed New Hampshire's Neediest
meta_description: Donate non-perishable food items or money to the New Hampshire Food Bank, and get a look inside our hangar at the same time.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: Help PlaneSense Feed New Hampshire’s Needy This Holiday Season
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
Help PlaneSense Feed New Hampshire’s Needy This Holiday Season
Behind the Scenes
•
December 13, 2017
Every day, approximately 10% of the population in New Hampshire is food insecure, meaning they do not know where and when their next meal may come. On Thursday, December 14, you will have the opportunity to help PlaneSense take concrete steps to fight hunger in our home state.
Tomorrow, we will be collecting non-perishable food items and monetary donations for needy families across the state to give to the New Hampshire Food Bank just in time for the holiday season. If you bring a donation to our front lobby by the flagpoles at
115 Flightline Road in Portsmouth, New Hampshire,
we’ll gratefully accept it. Not only that, but we’ll be happy to show you the hangar and take a photo of you with our planes!
On December 19th, we’ll be flying everything you donate over to Manchester and the
New Hampshire Food Bank
in one of our PC-12 aircraft. A year ago we collected over 7,000 pounds of food, and we’re looking to deliver even more this year. Donate to help us meet our goal and put food on the tables of your food insecure friends and neighbors.
To learn more about the New Hampshire Food Bank,
visit their website
. PlaneSense is proud to fight hunger in the state we are headquartered in, and we hope to see you at PlaneSense tomorrow from
9 a.m. to 5 p.m.!

title: Navigating Private Flights During Super Bowl Weekend
meta_description: Learn about how a share in the PlaneSense® fractional program can get you closer to the Super Bowl and other sporting events.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: Super Bowl Weekend Kicks Off the Crowds with an Influx of Private Jets Touching Down in the Vegas Area
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
Super Bowl Weekend Kicks Off the Crowds with an Influx of Private Jets Touching Down in the Vegas Area
Featured Destinations
•
February 5, 2024
With Super Bowl LVIII just days away, the Las Vegas area is bracing itself for an influx of football fans. Nearby airports are gearing up for the increase in flights by bringing in additional staff and equipment as they anticipate lengthy wait times and possible congestion during busy travel hours. Major airlines have responded to the surge in demand by increasing the number of flights available. There is also a notable influx of private jets aiming to reach the host city with utmost efficiency, and
PlaneSense® aircraft
are often part of the mix. Slots at local airports fill up fast after the Super Bowl teams have been named. During the 2023 Super Bowl weekend, airports in the Phoenix Metro area saw roughly 4,000 more take-offs and 1,100 private jets than usual.
Flying privately allows fans to avoid all the hassle of commercial flights – organizing transportation, early arrival at the terminal, and long security lines. Maneuvering through airport crowds can take away from the joy of experiencing the biggest football game of the year alongside friends and family.
Opting for a private flight presents a remarkable solution, as it alleviates many of the travel-related headaches commonly associated with commercial flights. Instead of enduring lengthy waits at airport terminals, PlaneSense® clients use private terminals where they can promptly hand off their luggage to the flight crew and take off within minutes of stepping onto the aircraft. Additionally, private travel allows fans the freedom to set their own arrival and departure times surrounding any event, unlike commercial airlines, which are bound by fixed schedules and limited destinations.
PlaneSense® share owners
have the added benefit of the Flight Operations Team to schedule their flights, reserve slots, and schedule crews to get them to and from the game.
But the Super Bowl is just the beginning of a busy year of large sporting events that draw a large contingency of private aircraft. The Masters, Kentucky Derby, The U.S. Open, collegiate championships, and various auto racing events are known to fill local airports with private jets. Aircraft, such as the Pilatus PC-12 and PC-24 jet in the PlaneSense® fleet allow fans to land closer to the sporting venue, making the most of their valuable time.
To learn more about how a share in the PlaneSense® program can get you closer to your favorite game, contact
sales@planesense.com
or call 866-214-1212.

title: PlaneSense, Inc Awarded Platinum Elite Status by ARGUS
meta_description: PlaneSense, Inc is one of only five aviation companies in the U.S. to be recognized with Platinum Elite Status for safety by ARGUS.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: PlaneSense® Fractional Program Awarded Platinum Elite Status by ARGUS
h2: Robust training
Operational Experience
Young, state-of-the-art aircraft
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
PlaneSense® Fractional Program Awarded Platinum Elite Status by ARGUS
Fractional Facts
•
August 22, 2023
PlaneSense, Inc is one of only five aviation companies in the U.S. to be recognized with Platinum Elite Status for
safety by ARGUS
, an independent organization that audits aviation companies on their
safety protocols
. While PlaneSense, Inc. just recently achieved this highest possible rating, it has consistently been awarded with ARGUS Platinum status since 2016 and is also an International Standard for Business Aircraft Operations (IS-BAO) registered company.
ARGUS conducts in-depth audits at regular intervals to ensure an operator’s Safety Management System (SMS) is strong and well-executed. To earn the newly designated Platinum Elite status, an operator must have fulfilled a number of requirements including an in-depth on-site audit, developed a strong functioning SMS and Emergency Response Plan, and participated in Maintenance and Operations reviews every two years.
An aviation Safety Management System is a formal set of processes and tools that guide and oversee an organization’s safety program and mitigate risk. PlaneSense, Inc. was founded in 1995 with an uncompromising commitment to safety as the highest priority. Having a strong SMS is just one part of a well-rounded focus on ensuring that clients, staff, and aircraft remain safe at all times. In addition to the protocols within PlaneSense’s SMS, there are many ways the company and its teams ensure that safety remains at the forefront of its operations every single day:
Robust training
PlaneSense
®
pilots
are recognized in the industry for their extensive training and expertise. Unlike some private flight providers, PlaneSense
®
pilots are staff of the company, not a third-party vendor. All training is overseen by PlaneSense, Inc. so the quality of training is consistent and thorough. All pilots in the program attend ground school training prior to flying clients. This includes instruction with an in-house flight simulator training device developed by FlightSafety International and PlaneSense. Pilots then take part in full-motion simulator training at one of FlightSafety International’s facilities. They participate in ongoing training throughout the year to ensure they remain current on the standards required of the PlaneSense
®
program.
Maintenance technicians for the PlaneSense
®
fleet also focus on safety within strict standards. They attend Pilatus, Williams, and Pratt & Whitney factory schools, and also follow a rigorous in-house training program. The maintenance team averages over 2,200 training hours each year. This focus allows technicians to have the highest level of expertise in the Pilatus aircraft. Atlas Aircraft Center, Inc. has received the FAA Diamond Award for Excellence every year since 2002. Atlas has also been recognized with the Avionics Training Excellence Award from the Aircraft Electronics Association (AEA) for many years.
Operational Experience
PlaneSense, Inc. manages one of the world’s largest commercial fleets of Pilatus PC-12 aircraft and Pilatus PC-24 jets. This means that its team has more operational experience with these aircraft than most operators, allowing for a strong focus on safety and quality.
Young, state-of-the-art aircraft
The PlaneSense
®
fleet is comprised of two industry-respected aircraft – the Pilatus PC-12 turboprop and the Pilatus PC-24 jet. The PC-12 continues to be one of the most sought after aircraft in the world, known for its craftmanship, reliability, efficiency, and versatility. The PC-24 jet was built with the same Swiss commitment to quality that has made the PC-12 so popular. While the aircraft are known for their longevity, PlaneSense, Inc. intentionally keeps the fleet young to provide clients and pilots with the most modern avionics and technology possible.
Every aspect of the PlaneSense
®
operation, including, scheduling and dispatch, flight operations, maintenance, training, and employment policies is conducted with safety in mind so that its fractional share owners have a safe, outstanding private flight experience. To learn more about the PlaneSense
®
program, call 866-214-1212 or reach out to
sales@planesense.com
.

title: PlaneSense Collects Donations for Holiday Food Drive
meta_description: This holiday food drive, consider donating to the NH Food Bank, an organization that feeds thousands of NH residents each year.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: PlaneSense Collects Donations for Annual Holiday Food Drive
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
PlaneSense Collects Donations for Annual Holiday Food Drive
Behind the Scenes
•
December 3, 2023
As the holidays quickly approach, PlaneSense encourages the Seacoast business community to get in the spirit of giving by taking part in our annual holiday food drive. Please join our staff, business partners, and local businesses on December 12th to make your non-perishable food donation.
Since 2014,
PlaneSense has donated over 6,400 meals to families through the New Hampshire Food Bank. Just last year, our community collected 2,456 pounds (2,047 meals) of food for the NH Food Bank. This year, we hope to donate even more meals to those in need.
Food insecurity affects one in every nine children here in the Granite State. These children do not know where or when they will get their next meal. With your food donations, you can help us make a difference in securing meals for children and adults alike. The New Hampshire Food Bank works with 425 partner agencies across the state to distribute food to those in need.
Help us make a difference to families in New Hampshire!
December 12, 2018
9 a.m. to 5 p.m.
PlaneSense, Inc.
115 Flightline Road
Pease International Tradeport
Those dropping off food will be acknowledged via social media and are invited to take a photo in front of one of our aircraft. We’ll fill a
PC-12 aircraft
with your donations and fly it to the NH Food Bank in Manchester on December 20th.
For more information, please call 603-501-7759.

title: PlaneSense | Navigating Summer Storms
meta_description: Safety is paramount for PlaneSense. Learn how we carefully asses, plan, and navigate summer storms before and during a flight.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: Navigating Summer Storms
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
Navigating Summer Storms
Behind the Scenes
•
July 14, 2017
The summer season
brings with it the possibility of thunderstorms, tornadoes, hurricanes, and other dangerous weather conditions. While weather can pass through a region quickly, it can leave a path of destruction and airport delays in its wake. These weather conditions often change rapidly and may gain intensity as they’re being pushed through by a cold front. When summer storms are passing near or over an airport, aircraft will not be permitted to take off or land until the storm has passed.
By constantly monitoring FAA NOTAMs, TFRs and weather, we try to stay in front of potential scheduling changes and anticipated travel inconveniences due to weather. We contact owners as soon as we learn about any factors, like summer storms, that may affect flights and offer alternatives, if available.

title: Private Flight Is Taking Over Super Bowl Travel | PlaneSense
meta_description: PlaneSense offers some statistics that make it clear that Super Bowl flights are in demand, and none more so than private flight options.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: Super Bowl Flights: How Private Flight Is Taking Over The Big Game
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
Super Bowl Flights: How Private Flight Is Taking Over The Big Game
Featured Destinations
•
February 1, 2022
When the New England Patriots and Philadelphia Eagles meet up in Minnesota for Super Bowl 52, thousands upon thousands of people will descend on the city to watch the game. This creates a huge demand for flights in and out of Minneapolis, where the game will be played in the brand new and impressive U.S. Bank Stadium. It also creates some travel headaches, and that in turn creates a huge opportunity for private flight.
A surprising number of those people will arrive via private flights. Per the
Minneapolis/St. Paul Business Journal
, as many as 1,500 private flights will land at area airports ahead of the Super Bowl, PlaneSense very much among them. While Delta has announced it will add 21 new commercial flights in the week leading up to the game for fans, those with the means to avoid major airports and long security lines are doing so. Most of those flights–many of which will be carrying senior NFL staff and celebrities–will come through a small handful of airports both within Minneapolis and just outside the city.
The benefits to avoiding commercial flight are obvious and varied. Being able to bypass long lines for security, avoiding long drives to and from your lodging, and potential delays are all hugely attractive, given how difficult it will be to get in and out of Minneapolis. And, of course, there’s the benefit of not being packed elbow-to-elbow with fans of your least favorite team, should it come to that. If you fly PlaneSense, for example, it’s just you, your pilot, and any friends and family members you take with you.
The Super Bowl kicks off on Sunday, February 4th at 6:30 p.m. EST. If you’re not able to travel to the game, it will be shown live on NBC.
Stay tuned for our next blog post, where we’ll break down some of the ways you can avoid the travel crunch in and out of Minneapolis. If you’re interested in learning
how fractional ownership
can get you to the Super Bowl other major sporting events comfortably and quietly, don’t hesitate to
reach out to us about our fractional ownership program
!

body_text:
PlaneSense Ranked as #1 Private Lift Provider
Industry Insights
•
October 4, 2024
Each year
Business Jet Traveler magazine (BJT)
surveys its readers on a variety of topics pertaining to private flying, including the evaluation of lift providers. Twelve operators made the 2024 list, but the PlaneSense®
fractional program
came out on top in five of the six categories, earning the highest score of 4.0 in three categories:
Overall satisfaction – 4.0
Customer service – 3.89
Value for price paid – 3.78
Peak day policies – 4.0
Availability of aircraft on short notice – 4.0
“I am thrilled that Business Jet Traveler readers ranked us top among all private aviation providers in its annual
survey
,” says George Antoniadis, President & CEO, PlaneSense, Inc. “For 30 years, all of us at PlaneSense have been committed to providing our clients with world class safety, service, and value. We continually strive to enhance our programs and service and look forward to many more opportunities to exceed the expectations of private flyers.”
Fractional Aircraft Owners Choose Pilatus
PlaneSense placed third in the remaining category, Aircraft model choices. The PlaneSense® fleet currently features 43 Pilatus
PC-12 turboprops
and 14
PC-24 jets
. Coincidentally, the PC-12 was ranked as the most sought-after single engine turboprop by BJT survey participants, and the PC-24 jet moved up to second place in the light jet category. Fractional owners have long recognized the aircraft in the PlaneSense® fleet for their versatility, reliability, large cabins, comfort, interior luggage space, and efficiency.
The PlaneSense® program serves the lower 48 states of the U.S., as well as Canada, Bermuda, the Bahamas, the Caribbean, Central America, and Mexico.
Experience the program
that is the top choice of private flyers like you! Contact us at 866.214.1212 or
sales@planesense.com
.

title: Celebrating 30 Years of Innovation in Private Aviation | PlaneSense
meta_description: Celebrating over 30 years of innovation. Since 1995 PlaneSense has been delivering fractional aviation excellence.
author: Clarissa Wright

h1: PlaneSense Celebrates 30 Years of Innovation in Private Aviation
h2: Second Oldest Fractional Aircraft Program Marks Three Decades of Dedication to Excellence, Safety, and Customer Service
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
PlaneSense Celebrates 30 Years of Innovation in Private Aviation
Behind the Scenes
,
News
•
September 9, 2025
Second Oldest Fractional Aircraft Program Marks Three Decades of Dedication to Excellence, Safety, and Customer Service
PlaneSense, a leading provider of
fractional aircraft ownership
, commemorates its 30th anniversary on September 9, 2025, marking three decades of transformative growth, innovation, and an unwavering commitment to client satisfaction within the private aviation industry. Since its inception in 1995, PlaneSense has redefined the standard for fractional aircraft programs,
consistently placing safety, reliability
, and personalized service at the heart of its operations.
“When we launched PlaneSense three decades ago, our vision was to offer a more intelligent and highly personal approach to private flying,” says George Antoniadis, Founder, President, and CEO,
PlaneSense, Inc
. “We believed clients deserved a sophisticated aircraft but also a trusted partner dedicated to fulfilling their flight needs with a cost-effective, safe, and service-focused solution. Thirty years later, that philosophy still drives every decision we make.”
The PlaneSense fractional program launched on September 9, 1995, with the delivery of the company’s
first Pilatus PC-12
, the 20th produced by Pilatus Aircraft Ltd. With its short field capabilities, flexibility to land on unpaved runways, comfortable cabin, interior cargo, and proven reliability, the PC-12 is a highly sought-after solution for business and leisure flights.
In 2015, PlaneSense took a bold step forward by launching its jet program, paving the way for the much-anticipated arrival of the world’s first Pilatus PC-24 jet in February 2018. Building on the renowned Swiss craftsmanship of the PC-12, the introduction of the PC-24 elevated the private flight experience for PlaneSense clients seeking a larger cabin and extended range. Like the PC-12, the PC-24 can operate from shorter runways, delivers outstanding reliability, and provides a spacious interior cargo area. The spacious cabin is noticeably larger than those found in other light jets, providing passengers with superior comfort and a more enjoyable flying experience. Today, PlaneSense operates the largest fleet of
PC-12 turboprops
and
PC-24 jets
in the United States.
“As we celebrate this milestone, I am reminded that innovation is in our DNA,” adds Antoniadis. “From pioneering new approaches for
fractional ownership
to embracing the latest advancements in aviation technology, we are constantly evolving to better serve our clients. Their trust empowers us to expand our offerings and set new standards.”
Over the past year, PlaneSense introduced a variety of new services and products that were enthusiastically received by clients. An exclusive collaboration with Jetfly extends seamless service to Europe and Northern Africa where PlaneSense clients can use their flight hours on Jetfly’s fleet of Pilatus aircraft. For even more flexibility, the company unveiled the PlaneSense Sourcing Solution, a service that enables clients to access reliable private flights across the U.S. and anywhere in the world, including aircraft beyond the PlaneSense fleet. While PlaneSense has long operated throughout the continental U.S., Canada, Bermuda, the Bahamas, the Caribbean, Central America, and Mexico, these strategic initiatives now expand the program’s footprint even further, delivering more value and convenience to clients wherever they need to fly, both nationally and internationally.
Furthering the company’s leading-edge approach, the CobaltPass offering introduced in 2025 redefines the conventional jet card model by allowing clients to fully customize their flight solutions across aircraft types and enjoy travel with highly competitive pricing, unparalleled flexibility, and no blackout dates. The CobaltPass jet card complements the established fractional program, opening up new ways for private flyers to take advantage of all that PlaneSense offers while providing exceptional access and convenience for a broad range of travel needs.
Thanks in part to its renowned service levels and comprehensive suite of tailored programs, PlaneSense has achieved an impressive 91% client retention rate, with some families now spanning three generations of PlaneSense share owners. And while PlaneSense is recognized for its unique offerings and performance, Antoniadis also attributes the company’s enduring success to its dedicated employees, whose passion and expertise have shaped PlaneSense as an industry leader.
“Our team is the backbone of PlaneSense,” says Antoniadis. “Their determination to go the extra mile for our clients sets PlaneSense apart.”
.
PlaneSense has built a reputation since 1995 for delivering tailored private flight solutions to clients seeking flexibility, safety, and exceptional service. PlaneSense, together with its sister company Atlas Aircraft Center, employs a dedicated team of 600 professionals, operates state-of-the-art maintenance facilities in Portsmouth and Boulder City, NV, and provides comprehensive flight training for its pilots in Portsmouth and Tampa, FL. The company’s approach to fractional aircraft ownership and jet card programs prioritizes reliability and client satisfaction, earning consistent recognition, including top operator in Overall Satisfaction, Customer Service, and Value for Price Paid in Business Jet Traveler Magazine’s 2024 survey. With the coveted
ARGUS Platinum Elite
safety ranking, PlaneSense stands among an exclusive group of operators worldwide. Operating throughout North America, the Caribbean, and beyond, PlaneSense enables clients to travel privately with ease. Fractional share owners and jet card holders benefit from a program designed to adapt to their needs, delivering a seamless and trusted flying experience. Discover more about
PlaneSense’s private aviation
solutions.
.
Media Coverage:
Aviation International News,
PlaneSense Celebrates Pearl Anniversary
Private Jet Card Comparisons
,
Pilatus PC-12, PC-24 operator PlaneSense marks 30
th
anniversary

Investors Hangout,
Celebrating 30 Years of Unmatched Excellence in Aviation

title: Forbes Explores PlaneSense Fractional Share Program
meta_description: Forbes Explores PlaneSense Fractional Share Program. Read our summary and expansion with jet cards with CEO George Antoniadis at Forbes!
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: Forbes Explores PlaneSense Fractional Share Program in Recent Articles
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
Forbes Explores PlaneSense Fractional Share Program in Recent Articles
News
•
April 16, 2018
Doug Gollan, seasoned luxury travel correspondent and private aviation solutions expert, recently sat down to speak with PlaneSense founder and CEO George Antoniadis for
an article for Forbes.com
about the
private jet industry
and the future of fractional ownership.
Gollan profiled PlaneSense for both the acclaimed online medium as well as his personal blog,
Private Jet Card Comparisons
. He outlined Antoniadis’ unique business plan for meeting the demands of this ever-growing market, and ensuring a high level of customer satisfaction at every step of the way.
The article for Forbes highlights Antoniadis’ distinct approach to the industry and explores the ins and outs of how the company functions and thrives, offering exclusive details about the customer base, the PlaneSense fleet of aircraft, and the fractional aircraft model itself. The company flies both Pilatus PC-12s and PC-24s, as well as Nextant jets, and last year flew to 48 states and much of the Caribbean.
“
For PlaneSense founder and CEO George A. Antoniadis,” writes Gollan, “the leap is less about dreams of getting bigger or unbridled ambitions for growth and more a response to his current customers who want to see the company provide a broader range of value-focused solutions.”
In both articles, Gollan emphasizes PlaneSense’s decision to refrain from selling jet cards and how this choice sets the company apart from other fractional programs. Putting the customers first and ensuring they always feel they are the company’s top priority is key to that approach.
“Antoniadis believes mixing
jet card memberships
and fractional customers creates a two-class system, something he isn’t interested in,” Gollan writes.
To learn more about PlaneSense’s role in the evolution of the private aviation industry, head over to Forbes.com to read Gollan’s article, or check out his blog for a
special feature
.

title: 8 Essential Tips For Private Travel | PlaneSense
meta_description: 8 essential tips for a stress-free private travel plan this holiday season with planesense fractional ownership.
author: Clarissa Wright

h1: 8 Essential Tips for Stress-Free Private Travel This Holiday Season
h2: 1. Choose Less Busy Airports
2. Find a Trusted Flight Provider
3. Understand the Pricing and Payment Terms
4. Select the Right Aircraft Type
5. Consider Fractional Ownership Over Charter
6. Plan for Potential Delays
7. Be Aware of Customs and Immigration Requirements
8. Pre-arrange Ground Transportation
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
8 Essential Tips for Stress-Free Private Travel This Holiday Season
Behind the Scenes
•
December 3, 2024
Navigating private travel during the busy holiday season can be challenging, as demand surges and airports become more and more crowded. If you’re planning on traveling during this very busy, possibly record breaking holiday season, you could find yourself in the hectic holiday shuffle. This might include
canceled flights
, weather related delays, and many other possible challenges which could be a recipe for stress. Here are some key tips to keep in mind for a
smoother travel experience
:
1. Choose Less Busy Airports
Opting for a smaller regional airport can help
avoid the congestion
of major commercial hubs, allowing holiday travelers to spend less time waiting in crowded security lines and more time celebrating with loved ones.
2. Find a Trusted Flight Provider
When selecting flight solutions, it’s important to assess the background and reputation of the different options available. How long has the operator been in business? What safety ratings have they earned? Reading reviews from private traveler publications, such as
Business Jet Traveler magazine’s annual reader’s survey
, can offer valuable insight into a flight provider’s safety and reliability.
3. Understand the Pricing and Payment Terms
Private flight rates can fluctuate significantly during the holiday season due to increased demand. It’s key to inquire about peak season surcharges and be aware of any hidden fees. With
fractional ownership
, monthly fees and hourly rates are known upfront, and typically, there are no additional fees to travel during peak periods.
4. Select the Right Aircraft Type
The ideal aircraft
depends on the nature of your trip. For regional flights with immediate family members, a single-engine turboprop like the
Pilatus PC-12
might be the ideal fit. For longer journeys or trips with additional children and pets, a light jet such as the
Pilatus PC-24
will provide additional space, comfort, and range. If you’re traveling with gifts, ski gear, or excessive luggage, be sure to select aircraft that offer ample, interior, climate controlled storage space, such as the Pilatus aircraft in the PlaneSense® fleet.
5. Consider Fractional Ownership Over Charter
Fractional ownership offers several advantages over traditional charter services, especially during busy travel periods. As a fractional owner in the
PlaneSense® program
, clients can rely on consistent, high-quality service, along with experienced crew members who prioritize passenger
safety
– ensuring that you and your family are in expert hands without
unreliable charter flight fees
that often fluctuate with demand.
Pilatus PC-24 Cargo Area
Comfortable Flights for the Whole Family
6. Plan for Potential Delays
Even with private travel, delays may still occur. It’s important to arrive ahead of departure time, and factor in extra time for traffic,
potential weather disruptions
, and Air Traffic Control (ATC) delays.
7. Be Aware of Customs and Immigration Requirements
If traveling internationally, take a moment to ensure all necessary documentation is in order. This includes passports, visas, and travel forms for minor children and pets. Preparing these details in advance will help
avoid last-minute stress.
8. Pre-arrange Ground Transportation
Whether it’s a rental car, chauffeur, family member, or shuttle, having ground transportation lined up in advance will save valuable time.
Take the first step toward a stress-free holiday season and discover how the PlaneSense®
fractional ownership program
can make your
private travel easier,
enjoyable, and more affordable. To learn more, call 866-214-1212 or email
sales@planesense.com
.

title: Private Flyers Rank PlaneSense First in Customer Service
meta_description: Private flyers ranked the PlaneSense® fractional program above the rest when it comes to providing customer service to its clients.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: Private Flyers Rank the PlaneSense Program First in Customer Service
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
Private Flyers Rank the PlaneSense Program First in Customer Service
Industry Insights
•
November 28, 2022
Private flyers recently ranked the PlaneSense® fractional program as a cut above the rest when it comes to providing outstanding customer service to its clients, a tough achievement at a time when air travel has come under great scrutiny. Customer service has been a topic of contention in the industry over the past 18 months because commercial airlines and private aviation have been substantially impacted by a worldwide pilot shortage, staffing challenges, and supply chain issues, resulting in delays, increased prices, and service interruptions. When the soaring demand for private flights is factored in, along with limited available options, it is becoming increasingly difficult to provide clients with the best customer service experience possible. Even with all this happening, the PlaneSense® fractional program has worked diligently to successfully fly above client expectations as proven in a recent survey of private flyers.
Business Jet Traveler magazine
recently asked readers to rank eleven lift services, several of which operate similarly to the PlaneSense® program. Among its closest competitors in the
survey
, the PlaneSense® program ranked number one in Customer Service, Aircraft Choices, Value for Price Paid, and Overall Satisfaction. As a program known for its reliability and value, the recognition is a well-appreciated honor.
Private flyers in the survey also ranked the
Pilatus PC-12
turboprop as the number one desired single turboprop. The
Pilatus PC-24 light jet
was also ranked as one of the most highly desired light jets, continuing to grow in popularity every year since it entered the market in 2018.
The PlaneSense® program
manages the world’s largest commercial fleet of Pilatus PC-12s and PC-24s, giving it more operational experience in the aircraft than other programs. Both aircraft are well known for their versatility, reliability, and efficient operation. Clients also appreciate the spacious and comfortable cabins and the generous interior cargo area, attributing to a great flight experience. The fleet is made up of newer aircraft to ensure clients have access to the most state-of-the-art technology and safety features. The average age of the fleet is under five years and is overseen by the company’s expert maintenance operations.
The importance of customer service
Ninety percent of Americans review customer service ratings when deciding what companies to do business with, and 58 percent of consumers will switch companies due to poor customer service. Often, smaller companies excel at providing a more personal customer experience whereas larger companies may fail because of scripted and almost robotic approaches to customer support. Since 89 percent of consumers are more likely to make a second purchase after experiencing positive customer service, smaller businesses may land bigger wins more consistently, increasing their sales and customer retention.
Exceptional customer service has been one of the PlaneSense® program’s highest priorities since its launch in 1995. As a result, the company has a high retention rate among its clients. In fact, a large percentage of PlaneSense® clients have been a part of the program for more than 10 years, with many enjoying more than 20 years of private flight services. Many PlaneSense® fractional share owners have added onto their existing shares from 1/8th to 3/16ths at a record-breaking rate in recent years. Additionally, clients regularly refer the program to their friends and colleagues who are also looking to fly privately.
A large part of the PlaneSense® program’s focus on customer service involves the pilots that operate the growing fleet. By providing high quality training, the company ensures its pilots are not only experts in the safe operation of the aircraft but are also providing outstanding customer service throughout clients’ flights. The dedicated team in the Flight Operations Center addresses each client’s personalized flight needs from the point of reservation to arrival at their destination.
To learn more about how the PlaneSense® program provides a complete and cohesive experience that
Flies Above Expectations
,
Call us today at 866-214-1212
or email sales@planesense.com.

title: George Antioniadis Living Legend Award | PlaneSense
meta_description: PlaneSense Founder, George Antoniadis was honored with the Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award at the Living Legends of Aviation Awards.
author: Clarissa Wright

h1: George Antoniadis Awarded the Kenn Ricci Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award at Living Legends of Aviation Event
h2: Pioneering PlaneSense®Founder Recognized for Transformative Impact on Private Aviation
Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
George Antoniadis Awarded the Kenn Ricci Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award at Living Legends of Aviation Event
News
•
February 4, 2026
©2026 Living Legends of Aviation / Brett Johnsen.
Pioneering PlaneSense
®
Founder Recognized for Transformative Impact on Private Aviation
PlaneSense Founder, President and CEO, George Antoniadis, was honored with the distinguished Kenn Ricci Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award at the 23rd Annual Living Legends of Aviation Awards. The event, hosted by actor John Travolta, the organization’s Official Ambassador of Aviation, took place on Friday, January 23, 2026, at the Beverly Hilton Hotel in California.
Antoniadis’ journey began as a young aviation enthusiast in Greece and evolved into a career defined by innovation, leadership, and a commitment to elevating private aviation standards. After earning an engineering degree from ETH in Zurich and an MBA from Harvard University, Antoniadis pursued his passion for aviation by obtaining an ATP pilot’s license. He honed his business and analytical expertise at McKinsey & Company before founding PlaneSense, Inc. in 1995, transforming his personal vision into a world-class aviation program. Under his leadership the Portsmouth, NH–based company has grown into one of the nation’s top fractional ownership programs, redefining private aviation with a specialized fleet, outstanding service, and a competitive value proposition.
George Antoniadis was recognized with the Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award by the Living Legends of Aviation
His contributions to the industry include helping to shape the modern regulatory framework for fractional ownership programs at a time when none existed. Antoniadis and his team assisted in organizing and convening the first Fractional Ownership Aviation Rulemaking Committee, bringing together operating companies, the FAA, manufacturers, and charter operators. Their work ultimately contributed to the creation of the landmark FAR 91K rule, which governs fractional operations today.
His forward-thinking leadership was also evident as PlaneSense expanded its fleet with the Pilatus PC-12, which was relatively unknown in the United States at the time. To ensure safe and effective integration of this aircraft into the national airspace system, he led an initiative to educate air traffic controllers on the PC-12’s capabilities, allowing the aircraft to reach its full operational potential and raising industry awareness.
George Antoniadis accepts his Lifetime Aviation Entrepreneur Award from Kenn Ricci of Flexjet, who nominated him for the honor. Copyright 2026 Living Legends of Aviation / Brett Johnsen.
Through dedication and a focus on service, safety, and value, Antoniadis’ company currently operates the world’s largest fleet of Pilatus PC-12 turboprops and PC-24 jets. Based in Portsmouth, New Hampshire, PlaneSense completed more than 47,800 flights in 2025 across the U.S., Canada, Bermuda, the Bahamas, the Caribbean, Central America, and Mexico. PlaneSense has grown to include more than 600 team members, two leading-edge maintenance facilities, and a comprehensive pilot training program.
“We grew methodically, ethically, and thoughtfully, relying on strong relationships across the aviation community and a deep understanding of our clients’ needs,”
Antoniadis remarked in his speech before a distinguished audience of celebrities and aviation leaders.
“Our success has always come from a commitment to safety and exceptional service.”
Antoniadis attributes the success of the PlaneSense fractional program to the team of more than 600 experts at the company, including the executive team that was in attendance to celebrate the award with him.
Guided by Antoniadis’ unwavering dedication to excellence, PlaneSense has always prioritized quality and safety across the operation, with a focus on comprehensive pilot training and rigorous maintenance standards. Over its 30+ year history, PlaneSense has expanded to include advanced, in-house pilot training programs with cutting-edge technologies and full flight simulators, plus world-class maintenance centers in Portsmouth, New Hampshire, and Boulder City, Nevada. By bringing these elements in-house, Antoniadis ensures superior oversight and operational excellence. His unique approach upholds the highest standards of safety, reliability, and quality, fuels sustainable growth, and cements PlaneSense as an industry leader.
“I would not have reached this point without the team that surrounds me. I am fortunate to work with an incredibly strong, energetic, and intelligent group that helped grow PlaneSense from a concept into the world class program it is today. I am proud to share this award with them and with all of our colleagues across the United States,”
Antoniadis said.
“Most importantly, I am deeply grateful to my family for their steadfast support and encouragement during this journey. Their understanding through the many days I am away from home has meant everything to me.”
Antoniadis is also committed to advancing education within the aviation sector, actively shaping the future for aspiring pilots and industry professionals. He is an advisor to the Aero Club of New England and leads the Board of the New England Aeronautical Institute, a New Hampshire-accredited remote learning college dedicated to expanding access to aeronautical education. Additionally, he serves as Chair of the Board of Trustees for Athens College, an elite K-12 school, where he supports initiatives that foster academic excellence and innovation.
Launching with one Pilatus PC-12 in 1995, PlaneSense now operates the world’s largest fleet of PC-12s. It’s versatility, comfort, and reliability make it a favorite among private flyers, including Antoniadis himself, a licensed ATP pilot, who can often be found behind the controls.
His enduring passion for flight, combined with his global outlook and innovative spirit, continues to inspire the private aviation community and drive progress for future generations. Even today, Antoniadis can often be found in the cockpit, personally piloting one of the company’s Pilatus PC-12 aircraft, demonstrating his hands-on leadership and unwavering love for aviation.

title: PlaneSense Fills Aircraft with Donations for NH Food Bank
meta_description: PlaneSense Inc. collected 3,192 pounds of food donations for the NH Food Bank as part of the company’s Annual Holiday Food Drive in December.
author: Tiffany Nelson

h1: PlaneSense, Inc. Fills Aircraft with Food Donations for NH Food Bank
h2: Contact Us For Your Private Flight Solution

body_text:
PlaneSense, Inc. Fills Aircraft with Food Donations for NH Food Bank
News
•
January 28, 2019
PlaneSense Inc. one of the nation’s largest
fractional aircraft ownership companies
, collected 3,192 pounds of food donations for the NH Food Bank through the generosity of many within the PlaneSense family as part of the company’s Annual Holiday Food Drive in December. The donations were loaded onto a Pilatus PC-12 aircraft and flown to Manchester-Boston Regional Airport by PlaneSense President and CEO, George Antoniadis, where it was met by New Hampshire Governor Chris Sununu, Congressman-elect Chris Pappas, NH Food Bank Executive Director, Eileen Liponis, and many others.
“Our annual holiday food drive is a meaningful way for us to help our neighbors and families in need across the state,” said Antoniadis. “The generosity of our staff and business partners was once again extraordinary. I’m pleased that Governor Sununu and Congressman-elect Pappas could join us this year to support the New Hampshire Food Bank.”
“Thanks to the charitable contributions made by PlaneSense, the New Hampshire Food Bank will receive thousands of pounds of food in much needed donations, and New Hampshire families will have a warm meal this holiday season,” said Governor Christopher Sununu. “Providing for our neighbors is a quintessential component of the New Hampshire spirit, and PlaneSense has put that principal into practice. I’m honored to have been able to support their efforts.”
Pictured left to right: NH Governor Chris Sununu; PlaneSense, Inc. President and CEO, George Antoniadis; Congressman Chris Pappas; NH Food Bank Executive Director, Eileen Liponis; and President, NH Grocer’s Association / Chair, NH Food Bank Advisory Committee, John Dumais.
Since beginning its annual holiday food drive in 2014, PlaneSense has collected more than 11,500 pounds of food, the equivalent of nearly 9,600 meals for families across New Hampshire.
One out of every nine people in New Hampshire lack reliable access to healthy food.
The New Hampshire Food Bank provides hunger relief to the thousands of people suffering from food insecurity every year through 400 community partnerships, food drives, and education programs.
“We applaud PlaneSense employees and their families, business partners and local companies for generously partnering with the NH Food Bank on a food drive,” said Eileen Liponis, Executive Director of the NH Food Bank. “The holidays and winter months can be a very difficult time for those who are faced with ‘Eat or Heat’ decisions by the limitations of their resources.”
Donations poured in this year from PlaneSense staff as well as business partners, including: Schrager Hampson Aviation Insurance, Baker Newman Noyes, Customers Bank, People’s Capital, USAIG, Port City Air, and Raka Creative.
To learn more about the NH Food Bank, its work and more ways to get involved, visit
nhfoodbank.org
.
Tom Blonski, President & CEO, Catholic Charities NH, and George Antoniadis, President and CEO, PlaneSense, Inc., help unload food donations.
